steps.title: Identify the core problem
steps.content: The strongest signal is the claim that “simply replicating a feature...may be insufficient to induce increased walking in other cities.” This establishes that copying one city's success cannot be counted on to automatically increase walking elsewhere.

steps.title: Match the problem to the purpose
steps.content:
The text explains this through Alfonzo: walking decisions are complex, involving multiple factors—not just architecture.

So the main purpose is to discuss why finding a reliable method to increase walking is difficult.

D captures this directly. A, B, and C either misidentify the focus (A on effects, B on comparison) or frame the text as solution-focused (C)—That matches choice D — D is correct.
